content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers . You want to convert the array AAA into a permutation of NNN integers. To do this, you can apply the following operation on array AAA **exactly once** .


Pick an integer XXX, where 1‚â§X‚â§2‚ãÖ1071 \leq X \leq 2\cdot 10^71‚â§X‚â§2‚ãÖ107. For each iii , 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N , replace AiA_{i}Ai‚Äã by Ai%XA_{i} \% XAi‚Äã%X.
Find out whether it is possible to convert AAA into a permutation of NNN integers. If it is possible, print `YES` **(all uppercase alphabets)** and any possible value of XXX for which it is possible. Otherwise, print `NO` **(all uppercase alphabets)**.

Note - An array of NNN integers is called a permutation if it contains all integers from 111  to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the required answer in a single line. If an answer exists, print `YES` and an integer XXX (1‚â§X‚â§2‚ãÖ107)(1 \leq X \leq 2 \cdot 10^7)(1‚â§X‚â§2‚ãÖ107) separated by a single space. Otherwise, print `NO`.


Note that the strings are **case-sensitive** and must be in **uppercase alphabets**.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all cases won't exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first case, choosing X=4X=4X=4 yields AAA as [2%4,7%4,1%4]=[2,3,1][ 2\% 4, 7\% 4 , 1\% 4] = [2,3,1][2%4,7%4,1%4]=[2,3,1] which is a permutation of N=3N=3N=3.
In the second case, we can't make AiA_iAi‚Äã = 111 for any iii and for any value of XXX.
